description.p: Word Hunter is a desktop language-learning workspace for reading real texts, saving vocabulary with context, and reviewing words later.
It supports imported text, PDFs, EPUB files, subtitles, public-domain book discovery, local vocabulary storage, spaced repetition, text to speech, and optional offline translation models.

screenshot.caption: Reading view with vocabulary highlights and word details

ul.li: Fixed Windows saves and startup recovery failing with access denied while synchronizing backup files.
Made Pocket clearly explain when a scanned PDF requires desktop OCR before synchronization.
Fixed the Android Import drawer closing whenever a user tapped one of its controls.
Included the OCR reliability, progress animation, synchronized counters, and sentence correction fixes from the withdrawn 1.0.1 and 1.0.2 builds.
Restored the live PaddleOCR progress animation with a clearer document-scanning indicator.
Changed quick PDF sentence correction to edit and replace only the selected sentence.
Added automatic CPU recovery when accelerated PDF OCR fails at runtime.
Kept library word counters stable while synchronized book text refreshes in the background.
Updated release workflows to supported GitHub Actions runtimes and corrected Rust toolchain setup.
Established a new compatibility baseline after breaking storage and synchronization changes.
Added durable per-record local storage, safer recovery, and deterministic cross-device conflict handling.
Added guided Syncthing setup on desktop and staged folder synchronization on Android.
Refreshed Windows, Android, and x86_64 Flatpak packaging, validation, and third-party license notices.
Added a managed cloud sync coordinator with a Flatpak Google Drive connector through bundled rclone; cloud pulls are staged and validated before merge, and the MVP does not delete remote files.
Recovered interrupted Google Drive authorization by clearing partial app-owned rclone remotes and showing a retryable sign-in state.
Retried transient Google Drive quota and rate-limit failures while preparing the cloud sync folder.
Kept the visible Google Drive connector status stable across local/background sync snapshots instead of falling back to not connected after a successful connection.
Removed the separate Discover language selector; book discovery now follows the active learning profile language on desktop and Android.
Fixed Discover source selection so switching to Wikipedia, Wikinews, or Wikisource is preserved across Flatpak/background sync snapshots.
Improved the Linux/Flatpak GTK titlebar workaround so close and maximize buttons keep receiving clicks in normal window mode.
Fixed Flatpak sync folder selection so the chosen folder is stored only after a successful sync and uses XDG config/data locations.
Fixed Flatpak/Linux OCR GPU platform detection for more reliable startup diagnostics.
Bundled WebGPU/Vulkan OCR acceleration with a safe CPU fallback, PDFium, and PaddleOCR models for scanned PDFs on Linux.
Refreshed Windows packaging so installer and portable builds include the required GNU runtime DLLs.
Added Linux Flatpak packaging with AppStream metadata and local install helpers.
Improved PDF reading with page-background overlays, selectable words, and a clean text mode.
Improved Android Pocket PDF rendering with zooming, horizontal panning, and text-layer spacing fixes.
Hardened local-first storage, backup, recovery, and sync compatibility paths.
